name: Code Security Best Practices
body:
Code Security Best Practices gives security and engineering teams a baseline for monitoring source repositories and native security findings in Cyscale.

This version focuses on GitHub App and GitLab OAuth based inventory, open dependency alerts, code scanning or SAST alerts, secret scanning or secret detection alerts, and GitHub default branch protection posture. GitLab connector sync imports accessible projects and GitLab-native dependency scanning, SAST, and secret detection vulnerabilities. Cyscale code scans for GitLab repositories are launched manually or for repositories that opt in to auto-scan on sync.

<Procedure slug="code-security-github-alerts"/>

<Procedure slug="code-security-github-repository-posture"/>

<Procedure slug="code-security-gitlab-alerts"/>

procedures.name: 1. GitHub Security Findings
procedures.body: Repositories should not carry unresolved GitHub-native security findings. Dependency vulnerabilities, code scanning results, and exposed secrets should be triaged, remediated, or explicitly dismissed with a documented reason.

procedures.name: 2. GitHub Repository Posture
procedures.body: Repositories should protect default branches and require reviewed, verified changes before merge.

procedures.name: 3. GitLab Security Findings
procedures.body: GitLab repositories should not carry unresolved GitLab-native vulnerability findings. Dependency scanning, SAST, and secret detection findings imported during connector sync should be triaged, remediated, or explicitly dismissed with a documented reason. Cyscale code scan coverage for GitLab repositories should also be reviewed from Code Security because scans are started manually or for repositories with auto-scan enabled.
